title: find_matches default limit=50 silently truncates and reports the truncated count
evidence: server.py:39 limit=50 default; server.py:56 returns count=len(matches) after repository.py:79-83 applies matches[:limit] — a query with >50 results gives no signal more exist
suggestion: Return a total/unbounded count (or has_more flag) separate from the page size, or expose limit prominently in the tool docstring

title: Aggregate statistics/biggest_wins double-count matches that overlap across source CSVs
evidence: repository.py:243 statistics() and repository.py:277 biggest_wins() aggregate over _filter_matches with no _dominant_source_only dedup, unlike standings() (repository.py:167); BR-Football-Dataset overlaps Brasileirao_Matches for many seasons, so avg-goals/home-win-rate can be skewed when unfiltered
suggestion: Apply the same dominant-source dedup used by standings(), or document that aggregates assume a single competition/source filter

title: Line coverage is moderate (46%) despite 50 passing tests
evidence: test_coverage=0.46 in scores.json; acceptance tests use small fixtures so many branches go unexercised — e.g. _to_int failure paths (data_loader.py:34-43), limit handling, empty-result stat branches (repository.py:247-259), and full-CSV parsing of files only lightly seeded (extended/cup)
suggestion: Add targeted unit tests for the loader/normalize edge cases and the empty/limit branches to lift coverage of the data layer

title: 6 ruff lint warnings (import ordering + line length)
evidence: ruff check reports I001 unsorted imports (tests/conftest.py:13) and E501 line-too-long (tests/conftest.py:85, tests/acceptance/test_statistics.py:14-15, repository.py:26, data_loader.py:16); code_quality=0.667 in scores.json reflects these
suggestion: Run ruff check --fix and wrap the long lines; all 6 are auto-fixable or trivial

title: Robust data-quality handling beyond the minimum spec
evidence: normalize.py handles accent stripping (strip_accents), state-suffix removal and parenthetical country codes (clean_team_name), competition canonicalization, and multi-format date parsing (parse_date); data_loader.py:217-228 dedups overlapping league fixtures across files — exercised by tests/acceptance/test_data_quality.py
suggestion: No action — noted as a strength for cross-run comparison
